_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-7 ALS to BLS Transfer of Care (Revised 2/24/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: ALS to BLS Transfer of Care
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-7: ALS to BLS Transfer of Care — MDHHS Approved 2/24/2023
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern the conditions under which an ALS unit may transfer care of a patient to a BLS unit for transport. ALS to BLS transfer must only occur when the patient's condition is appropriate for BLS transport and the patient does not require ongoing ALS intervention. MCAs must define the criteria and process for ALS to BLS transfer.

sections.title: Authorization
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-7: ALS to BLS — Authorization
points: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved ALS to BLS transfer of care
ALS to BLS transfer requires MCA-defined criteria to be met
Medical control authorization may be required per MCA protocol before ALS to BLS transfer
The ALS provider retains responsibility for the patient until formal transfer is complete

sections.title: Eligibility Criteria for ALS to BLS Transfer
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-7: ALS to BLS — Eligibility
points: Patient condition is stable and NOT expected to deteriorate during transport
No ongoing ALS interventions are required (IV access not needed, no ALS medications running, no advanced airway in place)
Patient does NOT have a time-sensitive condition (STEMI, stroke, major trauma, septic shock)
Vital signs are within normal limits and stable
Patient pain is adequately managed with no expected need for ALS pain management during transport
BLS transport is sufficient to reach the appropriate receiving facility

sections.title: Conditions That PROHIBIT ALS to BLS Transfer
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-7: ALS to BLS — Contraindications
points: Active cardiac dysrhythmia requiring monitoring or treatment
Advanced airway in place (ETT or SGA)
Ongoing IV medication administration
Hemodynamic instability
STEMI, stroke, or major trauma
Altered mental status or declining level of consciousness
Post-cardiac arrest (ROSC) patients
Any patient the ALS provider clinically assesses as requiring continued ALS monitoring

sections.title: Transfer Process
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-7: ALS to BLS — Transfer Process
points: ALS provider must conduct face-to-face verbal report with BLS crew including: patient name, age, chief complaint, assessment, vitals, interventions, response to treatment, and clinical rationale for BLS transfer
BLS crew must acknowledge receipt of report before ALS is released
Both units document transfer in respective PCRs
ALS provider documents clinical rationale for ALS to BLS transfer in PCR

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize ALS to BLS transfer and define eligibility criteria
options: Authorized — MCA criteria and process defined

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may require online medical control authorization before ALS to BLS transfer
options: Medical control authorization required for ALS to BLS transfer
Medical control authorization not required — ALS provider discretion per protocol

deterioration_flags: Patient deteriorates after ALS to BLS transfer — BLS crew must contact ALS/Medical Control immediately; initiate BLS protocols appropriate to condition
ALS provider has concern patient does not meet BLS transfer criteria — do NOT transfer; maintain ALS care; contact medical control
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — MCA must authorize and define criteria
Transfer requires: stable patient, no ongoing ALS interventions, no time-sensitive conditions, normal vitals
Prohibited: dysrhythmia, advanced airway, IV drips, hemodynamic instability, STEMI, stroke, trauma, ROSC, declining AMS
Face-to-face verbal report required before transfer
Both units document transfer in respective PCRs
ALS provider documents clinical rationale for transfer
